sections.paragraph: m. ἅπ. λεγόμ. Eze 7:11, according to the Hebrews, lamentation, for נֹהֶה (of the form קֹדֶשׁ), from the root נָהָה, but this is but little suited to the context, to which the LXX. gives a very suitable sense (Cod. Alex.) ὡραϊσμός, ornament, grace; in support of this compare the root נוּהַּ, Arab. نَاهَ to be conspicuous, to be magnificent.
